claims_not_yet_supported: `npx vibeshield install` — package.json `bin` is null; no installable CLI exists in the repo today.

Readiness checks #5-12 (rate limiting, per-user token/cost cap, streaming indicator, audit logging, privacy/data inventory, Apple/Google declaration readiness, UGC/DMCA workflow) — src/scan.ts implements NONE of these; they are spec/roadmap, not live checks.

Pitch line 'the same assessment also checks rate limits, per-user AI budgets, specific error handling, streaming UX, audit logs, privacy declarations, and relevant UGC or platform obligations' — overstates current capability; must be a static, clearly-labeled readiness summary or roadmap, never presented as a live result.
Real draggable browser Shield Pill / browser plugin with edge snap — CURRENT_STATE.md cuts real MV3 extension packaging; only the in-page demo.html widget exists.
GitHub Action 'generated by vibeshield install' — a CI workflow exists in-repo (.github/workflows/vibeshield.yml) but is not produced by any install command.
required_integration_owner_actions: Claude writes and pushes these two coordination review files to origin/main so the pitch automation's clone can read them.

Pitch automation MUST run in its OWN isolated clone (e.g. C:/Users/Admin/Desktop/vibeshield-pitch) on branch codex/live-pitch-experience-v2 branched off origin/main — never the live working copy (C:/Users/Admin/Desktop/vibeshield runs the daemon + integration loop), and never a checkout shared with codex/uiux-auto.

All wiring into web/app.html|demo.html|judge.html and any src/** (sponsor adapters, a real `vibeshield` bin/CLI) stays with Claude; Codex files an integration request in docs/pitch-v2/CLAUDE_INTEGRATION_REQUEST.md instead of editing those files.
Claude's integration loop reviews and decides any merge of codex/live-pitch-experience-v2; Codex must not merge its own branch.
Building the actual Judge Mode / Mode-1 / Mode-2 UI belongs to the codex/uiux-auto automation (ui-next/**). The pitch automation must CONSUME that UI, not rebuild a packages/judge-mode-v2.
supersedes_tasks: The UI-IMPLEMENTATION portions of this automation (§2.4 Judge Mode state machine, §2.5 components, §2.6 animation work, as IMPLEMENT_DELTA into packages/judge-mode-v2) are superseded by the codex/uiux-auto automation. This automation retains only the PITCH/SPEC/DOCS workstream.
reason: No active overlapping Codex worker exists: the coordination premise (PROMPT C codex/ui-judge-mode, PROMPT E codex/docs-qanda, OVERNIGHT_STATE.md, the four docs/CODEX_*_HANDOFF.md, VIBESHIELD_DUAL_AGENT_OVERNIGHT_MASTER.md, VIBESHIELD_STUDIO_MASTER_PROMPT_V4_RESEARCH_LOOPS.md) references files and branches that do NOT exist (repo has only main + overnight-base + origin/main; zero codex/* branches; one worktree). The genuine, current conflict is with the codex/uiux-auto automation just registered (CODEX_UIUX_LOOP.md / CODEX_UIUX_MISSION.md), which already owns Judge-Mode and Mode-1/Mode-2 UI building in ui-next/**; allowing this automation to IMPLEMENT_DELTA a packages/judge-mode-v2 would create a duplicate, competing Judge Mode and risk a shared-checkout collision. The prompt itself is safety-conscious (forbids daemon/guard/trace/scanner/schema edits, forbids Codex self-merge, and its legal-claim matrix is consistent with our 'risk indicator, not legal advice' honesty rule), so it is SAFE to run in SPEC_ONLY producing docs/pitch-v2/** + tests/pitch-spec/** only, in its OWN isolated clone, branched off origin/main. IMPLEMENT_DELTA is withheld until uiux-auto's UI exists and Claude confirms no duplication. Several pitch claims (npx vibeshield install, readiness checks #5-12, a real browser plugin) are not supported today and must remain explicit, labeled placeholders, never shown as live.
